title: 10 best herbal capsules for indigestion

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsules helps with indigestion because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm the digestive tract. The herbal extract in licorice root capsules reduces inflammation and irritation in the stomach lining, alleviating symptoms such as bloating, gas, and cramps. Additionally, licorice root has a unique compound called glycyrrhizin, which helps to strengthen the mucous membrane in the stomach, preventing acid reflux and heartburn from occurring.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which helps to reduce inflammation and discomfort in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of indigestion such as bloating and cramps.

Flavonoids: Licorice root contains flavonoids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that help to protect the stomach lining from damage and reduce inflammation caused by acid reflux and other digestive issues.
Licoricidin: This compound has been shown to have a direct effect on the digestive system, helping to soothe and calm the gut, reducing symptoms of indigestion such as heartburn and nausea.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: A triterpenoid saponin, glycyrrhizin has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate indigestion symptoms.

Liquiritigenin: A flavonoid, liquiritigenin has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help protect the stomach lining from damage, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of indigestion.
Licoricidin: A phenolic compound, licoricidin has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help reduce inflammation and kill bacteria that may be contributing to indigestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain a high concentration of glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps alleviate indigestion symptoms.
Leaves: Although not as widely used as roots, leaves are sometimes used for their medicinal properties and as a substitute for roots in some formulations.
Seeds: While not as common, some herbal supplements may use seeds for their potential health benefits, although their effectiveness is less studied compared to roots.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots from a reliable source and clean them thoroughly.
Grind the roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix 10g of the powdered root with 5g of starch and 5g of gelatin in a bowl.
Fill a capsule machine with the mixture and produce 100 capsules in 10 minutes.
Label and store the capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Caraway capsules helps with indigestion because they contain a natural compound called carvone, which has been traditionally used to alleviate digestive issues. When taken in capsule form, carvone stimulates the muscles in the stomach and intestines, helping to break down food more efficiently and reduce symptoms of bloating, discomfort, and nausea associated with indigestion. Additionally, caraway's anti-inflammatory properties help soothe the digestive tract, further alleviating discomfort and promoting a healthy gut environment.
remedy_properties: Carvone content: Caraway capsules contain a significant amount of carvone, a natural compound that has been shown to relax the muscles in the digestive tract and reduce spasms, thus alleviating symptoms of indigestion.

Antispasmodic properties: The active ingredients in caraway capsules have antispasmodic properties, which help to relieve cramps and spasms in the stomach and intestines, reducing discomfort and pain associated with indigestion.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Caraway capsules also possess anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the digestive tract, thereby alleviating symptoms of indigestion such as bloating, gas, and abdominal pain.
remedy_constituents: Carvone: Carvone is a terpene that helps with indigestion by reducing spasms in the digestive tract and alleviating symptoms of nausea and vomiting.
Limocine: Limocine is a phenolic compound that has anti-inflammatory properties, which helps to reduce inflammation in the digestive system and alleviate symptoms of indigestion.
Bornyl acetate: Bornyl acetate is a terpene that has a calming effect on the digestive system, reducing spasms and inflammation, and helping to alleviate symptoms of indigestion.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They are used to make capsules for indigestion because they contain compounds like carvone, which have anti-inflammatory and carminative properties that help alleviate indigestion and digestive issues.

Roots: They are used to make capsules for indigestion because they have been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help soothe the digestive system and reduce inflammation.

Leaves: They are used to make capsules for indigestion because they contain compounds like carvone and limonene, which have been traditionally used to treat various digestive issues, including indigestion and bloating.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of dried carum carvi roots from a reputable source at any time of year.
Dry the harvested roots further in a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 2 hours.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder for 30 seconds.
Mix the ground powder with 30g of gelatin and 10g of glycerin in a bowl for 2 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 0.5g of the powder mixture each and seal tightly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile capsules helps with indigestion because of its soothing and calming properties, which can ease digestive discomfort and promote a healthy gut. The capsules contain chamomile extract that has been used for centuries to calm the stomach and alleviate symptoms of indigestion such as bloating, gas, and stomach cramps. By promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation in the digestive tract, chamomile capsules can help regulate digestion and reduce symptoms associated with indigestion, allowing for a more comfortable and peaceful digestive experience.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Chamomile capsules help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps that can contribute to indigestion, and promoting a smooth digestion process.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory effects can help to reduce inflammation in the digestive system, which is often associated with indigestion symptoms such as bloating and discomfort.

Soothing and calming effects: The calming compounds present in chamomile capsules have a sedative effect on the digestive system, helping to calm the nerves and promote a sense of relaxation, which can help to alleviate stress-related indigestion.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: This flavonoid helps with indigestion by reducing inflammation in the digestive tract and promoting relaxation, which can alleviate symptoms of indigestion and discomfort.
Chamazulene: This sesquiterpene lactone has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe the digestive tract and reduce inflammation, making it easier to manage indigestion symptoms.

Luteolin: This flavonoid has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that help reduce inflammation and muscle spasms in the digestive tract, which can alleviate symptoms of indigestion and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Used due to their high apigenin and bisabolol content, which helps to soothe the digestive system and reduce inflammation.
Seeds: Utilized for their carminative properties, which aid in relieving gas and bloating associated with indigestion.
Leaves: Employed for their anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which help to calm digestive issues and reduce discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers and set aside for 30 minutes.
Crush the dried flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Sift 1 tablespoon of the powder through a fine mesh sieve into a bowl for 5 minutes.
Mix 1 teaspoon of the sifted powder with 1/4 teaspoon of vegetable cellulose in a bowl for 2 minutes.
Fill a capsule machine with the powder mixture and encapsulate according to manufacturer's instructions for 5 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger capsules helps with indigestion because they contain concentrated amounts of ginger's natural digestive properties. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerol and shogaol, have been shown to reduce nausea, alleviate bloating, and soothe the stomach lining, making it easier for food to digest. As a result, herbal ginger capsules can quickly relieve symptoms of indigestion, such as discomfort, heartburn, and abdominal pain, providing fast and effective relief from digestive discomfort.
remedy_properties: Gingerols and Shogaols: These bioactive compounds have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, which help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of indigestion such as bloating, cramps, and nausea.
Methyl Salicylate: This natural compound has analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to soothe the stomach and intestines, reducing discomfort and pain associated with indigestion.

Volatile Oils: The volatile oils present in ginger capsules, such as cineole and zingiberene, have carminative properties, which means they can help to expel gas from the digestive system, relieving bloating and flatulence.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These sesquiterpene compounds help with indigestion by reducing inflammation in the digestive tract and relieving nausea and vomiting associated with indigestion.
Shogaols: These compounds, similar to gingerols, help with indigestion by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and reducing stomach contractions that can exacerbate indigestion.

Zingiberene: This sesquiterpene compound helps with indigestion by relaxing the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and inflammation, and alleviating symptoms of indigestion such as bloating and cramps.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the most commonly used part for making capsules due to their high concentration of gingerols and shogaols, which have potent anti-inflammatory and digestive properties.
Stems: Although not as commonly used as rhyzomes, the stems of Zingiber officinale may also be used in capsule form for their potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Leaves: Some products may use the leaves of Zingiber officinale in capsule form, but this is less common than using the rhyzomes or stems.
remedy_recipe: Dry the fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes at 50 degrees celsius for 24 hours to preserve the active compounds.
Grind the dried rhizomes into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or a coffee grinder.
Mix 500 milligrams of the powder with 500 milligrams of cellulose in a ratio to fill the capsules.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the powder mixture using a capsule filling machine or a small spoon.
Store the herbal zingiber officinale capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel capsules helps with indigestion because they contain a natural compound called anethole, which has been shown to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract. This anti-inflammatory property helps to soothe and calm the stomach lining, reducing discomfort and cramps associated with indigestion. Additionally, fennel's carminative properties help to expel gas and alleviate bloating, further relieving symptoms of indigestion. By taking herbal fennel capsules, individuals can promote a healthy digestive system and enjoy improved digestion and reduced discomfort.
remedy_properties: Carminative property: Fennel's carminative property helps to prevent the formation of gas in the digestive tract, which can contribute to bloating and discomfort associated with indigestion.
Anti-inflammatory property: Fennel's anti-inflammatory property helps to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can alleviate symptoms of indigestion such as abdominal pain and cramping.

Digestive enzyme stimulant property: Fennel's ability to stimulate digestive enzymes can help to improve digestion and relieve symptoms of indigestion by breaking down proteins, carbohydrates, and fats more efficiently.
remedy_constituents: Fenchone: This terpene acts as an antispasmodic, helping to relax the muscles in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of indigestion such as cramps and spasms.
Anethole: This phenol has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help reduce inflammation and ease muscle spasms in the digestive tract, providing relief from indigestion.

Ferulic acid: This phenolic acid has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help protect the digestive tract from oxidative stress and inflammation, thereby reducing the risk and severity of indigestion.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used due to their high content of volatile oils, particularly anethole, which has anti-inflammatory and carminative properties that help alleviate indigestion symptoms.
Seeds: The seeds are used because they contain a higher concentration of essential oils, including anethole, and are often dried and powdered to make capsules that help soothe digestive issues.
Stems: The stems are used due to their ability to infuse into teas and capsules, providing a gentle, yet effective, way to relieve indigestion symptoms through the release of volatile oils.
remedy_recipe: Harvest foeniculum vulgare roots and leaves in early morning after dew has evaporated for maximum potency retention.
Dry the harvested plant material in a temperature-controlled environment at 50 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder at 1000 rpm for 5 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 400 milligrams of the powder and seal immediately to preserve potency.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

The capsules stimulate digestion and relieve bloating, cramps, and gas by relaxing the muscles in the digestive tract. remedy_parts: Seeds: The seeds are the most commonly used part of Coriandrum sativum for making capsules for indigestion due to their high oil content, which provides digestive benefits.
Leaves: The leaves are often used in herbal remedies for indigestion as they contain compounds that aid in digestion and alleviate symptoms.
Roots: The roots of Coriandrum sativum are sometimes used to make capsules for indigestion due to their ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate digestive issues.
remedy_recipe: Dry the coriandrum sativum leaves and flowers in a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 2 hours.
Grind 10g of dried coriandrum sativum leaves and flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix the ground coriandrum sativum powder with 2g of gelatin in a small bowl.
Fill 100 empty capsules with 200mg of the coriandrum sativum mixture.
Store the herbal coriandrum sativum capsules in an airtight container at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ceylon cinnamon capsules helps with indigestion because of its unique properties that help soothe the digestive system. The active compounds in Ceylon cinnamon, such as cinnamaldehyde and cinnamic acid, have natural anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of indigestion, bloating, and discomfort. Additionally, Ceylon cinnamon capsules can also improve digestion by stimulating the production of gastric juices and increasing blood flow to the stomach lining, helping to break down food more efficiently and preventing digestive issues.
remedy_properties: Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Ceylon cinnamon's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as cinnamaldehyde and epicatechin, help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of indigestion and discomfort.

Carminative Properties: The volatile oils present in Ceylon cinnamon capsules, like cinnamic acid, have natural carminative properties that help relieve gas, bloating, and flatulence associated with indigestion.

remedy_constituents: Cinnamaldehyde: This terpene acts as a carminative, helping to reduce bloating and gas by inhibiting the release of gas-producing compounds in the gut, providing relief from indigestion.
Cinnamic acid: This phenolic acid has anti-inflammatory properties, reducing inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviating symptoms of indigestion such as abdominal pain and nausea.
Coumarin: This phenolic compound has a calming effect on the digestive system, reducing spasms and cramps in the stomach and intestines, which can contribute to indigestion and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes are also used due to their high oil content, which includes cinnamaldehyde, providing relief from indigestion and other digestive problems.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried cinnamomum verum bark from mature trees with a rich aromatic flavor.
Grind the dried bark into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder for 10 minutes.
Mix 100g of gelatin with 200ml of distilled water in a saucepan and heat for 5 minutes.
Fill gelatin capsules with 0.5g of the powdered cinnamomum verum and seal immediately.
Store the capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 years.

remedy_parts: Roots: Piper methysticum roots are commonly used due to their bioactive compounds, including kavalactones, which have been shown to aid in digestion and alleviate indigestion symptoms.

Leaves: Piper methysticum leaves contain a mixture of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, that may help soothe and calm digestive issues, making them a potential candidate for indigestion capsules.

Stems: Piper methysticum stems also contain bioactive compounds like kavalactones and flavonoids, which may help alleviate digestive discomfort and support digestive health, making them a suitable option for indigestion capsules.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 25 grams of dried piper methysticum root and clean it thoroughly to remove any impurities.
Grind the cleaned piper methysticum root into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice grinder.
Mix 2 grams of the ground piper methysticum powder with 8 grams of cellulose powder in a bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the mixture from step 3 and seal them securely.
Store the finished piper methysticum capsules in a cool dry place for up to 12 months.

The calming properties of beach rose, also known as Rosa rugosa, help to reduce inflammation in the stomach and intestines, making it an effective remedy for indigestion caused by stress, eating too quickly or spicy foods. remedy_recipe: Harvest rosa rugosa flowers and leaves by cutting them at the base of the plant with sharp gardening shears.
Dry the harvested plant material by spreading it out in a single layer on a paper bag for 2 weeks.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 30 seconds.
Mix the powdered plant material with gelatin powder in a 3:1 ratio according to the product label and fill empty gelatin capsules.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

This provides quick relief from symptoms like bloating, discomfort, and heartburn, allowing individuals to feel more comfortable and at ease. Buds: They are used to make capsules for indigestion due to their ability to soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation.

remedy_recipe: Gather 250 grams of dried tilia europaea flowers and leaves from a reputable supplier.
Grind 150 grams of the dried tilia europaea flowers and leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix 150 grams of the powdered tilia europaea with 150 grams of vegetable cellulose powder to fill capsules.
Fill 100 capsules with the tilia europaea and vegetable cellulose mixture using a capsule filling machine.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe capsules helps with indigestion because they contain a concentrated extract of aloe vera gel, which has natural soothing properties that calm irritated stomach linings. The capsicum content in aloe stimulates digestion and relieves symptoms such as bloating, gas, and heartburn. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory compounds in aloe reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, allowing food to pass through smoothly and preventing discomfort. This natural remedy provides relief from indigestion and promotes overall digestive health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Aloe vera contains anti-inflammatory compounds like aloin, aloe-emodin, and chromone derivatives that reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of indigestion such as bloating, cramps, and discomfort.

Soothing mucilage: The gel inside aloe leaves contains a thick, protective substance called mucilage that forms a barrier on the stomach lining, soothing irritated tissues and reducing acid reflux, heartburn, and inflammation.

Digestive enzymes: Aloe vera also contains various digestive enzymes like lipase, amylase, and protease that help break down fats, carbohydrates, and proteins, promoting healthy digestion and reducing symptoms of indigestion such as bloating, gas, and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants, aloe leaves help soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation.
Roots: Known for their anti-inflammatory properties, aloe roots can aid in reducing symptoms of indigestion and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 200g of fresh aloe vera gel from mature leaves.
Filter the gel through cheesecloth to remove impurities and excess pulp.
Add 50ml of distilled water to the filtered gel and stir well.
Fill clean gelatin capsules with 500mg of the prepared gel mixture and seal.
Store the finished capsules in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Nutmeg capsules helps with indigestion because of its natural ability to soothe and calm digestive issues. The active compound, myristicin, has been shown to relax the muscles in the stomach and intestines, reducing inflammation and cramping. Additionally, nutmeg's warm and calming properties help to ease spasms and discomfort associated with indigestion, providing fast and effective relief from bloating, gas, and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Myristicin: A chemical compound found in nutmeg, myristicin has been shown to reduce inflammation and spasms in the digestive tract, helping to alleviate symptoms of indigestion such as bloating and cramps.

Methysticin: Another compound found in nutmeg, methysticin is believed to have a relaxing effect on the muscles in the stomach, which can help to ease symptoms of indigestion such as nausea and vomiting.

Oleoresins: The oleoresins present in nutmeg capsules are thought to stimulate digestion and improve the absorption of nutrients, which can help to alleviate symptoms of indigestion by reducing digestive discomfort and improving overall gut health.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing digestive issues and alleviating symptoms of indigestion.
Rhyzomes: Known for their carminative properties, which help to relieve gas, bloating, and discomfort associated with indigestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of dried myristica fragrans seeds and sift them through a fine-mesh sieve to remove impurities.
Grind the sifted myristica fragrans seeds into a coarse powder using a mortar and pestle for about 10 minutes.
Mix the ground myristica fragrans powder with 250 grams of tapioca starch in a bowl until well combined.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with a mixture of 500 milligrams of the myristica fragrans-tapioca starch blend and 50 milliliters of purified water using a capsule filler machine for about 5 minutes.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 years or in the refrigerator for up to 1 year.

remedy_recipe: Measure 250 grams of dried santalum album leaves into a mortar and pestle.
Add 1000 milliliters of distilled water to the mixture and let it steep for 30 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.
Dry the extracted liquid using a rotary evaporator at 40°c for 60 minutes or until a thick paste forms.
Fill gelatin capsules with 500 milligrams of the resulting powder and store in an airtight container for up to six months.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves, ensure they are free of debris and moisture.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator at 60°f for 24 hours or air drying.
Crush dried melissa officinalis leaves into fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder.
Mix 1 teaspoon of crushed melissa officinalis powder with 100ml of water to create an herbal infusion, steeping for 10 minutes.
Fill gelatin capsules with the herbal infusion mixture, ensuring each capsule contains approximately 250mg of melissa officinalis.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric capsules helps with indigestion because they contain curcumin, a potent compound that has natural anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Curcumin works to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of indigestion such as bloating, gas, and discomfort. Additionally, it enhances digestion by increasing the production of stomach acid and bile, which helps break down food more efficiently. By taking turmeric capsules regularly, individuals can experience relief from recurring indigestion episodes and enjoy a healthier digestive system.
remedy_properties: Curcumin: Has potent anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of indigestion such as bloating, gas, and stomach pain.

Ciclooxegnase-2 (COX-2) inhibition: Inhibits the production of prostaglandins, which are hormones that contribute to inflammation and pain in the digestive system, thereby reducing indigestion symptoms.
Gastroprotective properties: Has been shown to protect the mucous membrane lining the stomach from damage caused by acid reflux and other factors, reducing the risk of gastritis and esophagitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the turmeric plant contain a high concentration of curcuminoids, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate symptoms of indigestion.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes, or underground stems, of the turmeric plant are the part most commonly used in traditional medicine for their digestive benefits, including reducing inflammation and relieving stomach discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Source high-quality curcuma longa powder of at least 95% curcuminoids content.
Measure out 250 milligrams of curcuma longa powder per capsule using a digital scale.
Add a lubricant such as magnesium stearate to the powder mixture in a ratio of 1:5.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the powder mixture and seal them immediately to prevent oxidation.
Store the finished capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood capsules helps with indigestion because of its natural ability to soothe the digestive tract. The herb's bitter compounds stimulate digestion, increasing the production of bile and stomach acid to break down food more efficiently. This can help alleviate symptoms such as bloating, cramps, and discomfort associated with indigestion. Additionally, wormwood has anti-inflammatory properties that may reduce inflammation in the gut, further contributing to its digestive benefits. By supporting healthy digestion, wormwood capsules can provide fast and effective relief from indigestion symptoms.
remedy_properties: Irinoid compounds: Wormwood's irinoid compounds, such as absinthin, have been shown to relax the muscles in the digestive tract and reduce inflammation, which can help alleviate symptoms of indigestion like cramps and bloating.

Terpenoids: The terpenoids present in wormwood capsules, including camphor and thujone, have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that can help soothe the digestive system and reduce spasms in the stomach and intestines, leading to improved digestion and reduced indigestion symptoms.

Essential oils: Wormwood's essential oils, including camphor and bornyl acetate, contain anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help calm the digestive tract and reduce the growth of harmful bacteria or fungi that may be contributing to indigestion and other digestive issues.
remedy_parts: Roots: Wormwood roots are rich in bioactive compounds like sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, which help reduce inflammation and improve digestion.
Barks: The barks of wormwood contain tannins and phenolic acids that aid in soothing the digestive tract and reducing symptoms of indigestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried artemisia absinthium leaves measure 2 tablespoons.
Dry the harvested leaves in a low-temperature oven at 150°f for 6 hours.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered artemisia absinthium with 500 milligrams of gelatin capsules and fill them by hand.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hawthorn capsules helps with indigestion because they contain flavonoids and oligomeric proanthocyanidins, which have a soothing effect on the digestive tract. These bioactive compounds help to reduce inflammation and relax the muscles in the stomach and intestines, alleviating symptoms of indigestion such as bloating, cramps, and discomfort. Additionally, hawthorn's natural anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, further easing digestion and reducing symptoms of indigestion. This results in a sense of relief and comfort for those experiencing indigestion.
remedy_properties: Vasodilation: Hawthorn's flavonoids can relax blood vessels, which helps reduce inflammation and alleviate digestive discomfort associated with indigestion, making it easier to digest food.
Antioxidant activity: Hawthorn's antioxidants can neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress in the gut, which can contribute to indigestion symptoms such as bloating, cramps, and diarrhea.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Hawthorn's flavonoids and procyanidins have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help soothe the digestive tract and reduce inflammation that may be contributing to indigestion symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Hawthorn roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of indigestion.
Barks: The barks of hawthorn contain flavonoids and anthocyanins that help soothe digestive issues and reduce oxidative stress associated with indigestion.
remedy_recipe: Collect dried crataegus monogyna leaves and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Measure out 250mg of the powdered herb and mix it with 500mg of gelatin in a small bowl.
Add 100ml of distilled water to the mixture and stir until the gelatin is fully dissolved taking approximately 5 minutes.
Pour the mixture into empty gelatin capsules and seal them immediately using an automated capsule filler for 2 hours.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature away from direct sunlight for up to 6 months.

intro_desc: Herbal capsules for indigestion are a natural remedy designed to alleviate discomfort, bloating, and digestive issues caused by eating spicy or fatty foods. These capsules typically contain herbs such as peppermint, ginger, and chamomile, which work together to soothe the digestive tract and reduce inflammation. Examples of herbal capsules that help with indigestion include Digestive Enzymes by NOW Foods and Nature's Way Senna Leaf Capsules. By taking these capsules, people can enjoy improved digestion, reduced bloating, and a sense of relief from discomfort, allowing them to live a healthier and more comfortable life.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to indigestion that are treated with herbal capsules are bloating, flatulence, and gastritis. Herbal supplements such as peppermint, ginger, and turmeric capsules can help alleviate these conditions by soothing the digestive tract, reducing inflammation, and improving digestion. Additionally, herbs like licorice root, chamomile, and fennel may also be used to treat stomach ulcers, heartburn, and irritable bowel syndrome (IBS).
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal capsules that help with indigestion is a blend of peppermint, ginger, and licorice root. Peppermint helps to ease digestion and reduce nausea, while ginger has natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the stomach. Licorice root, on the other hand, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that protect the stomach lining and promote healing. Taking these capsules together can provide relief from indigestion symptoms, such as heartburn, bloating, and discomfort. Consult a healthcare professional before taking any herbal supplements.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Explore (New York, N.Y.), herbal capsules, specifically Zataria-Trachyspermum (ZT), were found to be effective in alleviating indigestion symptoms in patients with Irritable Bowel Syndrome (IBS). The study involved 50 patients who were divided into three groups and treated with either ZT capsules, a placebo, or mebeverine capsules for 4 weeks. The results showed that after the fourth week of intervention, symptoms of pain, bloating, and reflux, which are common indicators of indigestion, showed a significant decrease in the ZT group compared to the placebo and mebeverine groups (P < 0.05).
